extension.valueString: Term gestation (GA unspecified), recurrent late decelerations on CTG over 20 min, light vaginal bleeding, mother hemodynamically stable — fetal compromise concern; ANC.B5.DE3 (bleeding) positive + non-reassuring fetal monitoring (possible C-section indication)

[{"content": "Late decelerations on the fetal heart monitor not sure if emergent C-section is needed could it be a normal variant?", "role": "user"}, {"content": "Late decelerations can sometimes signal uteroplacental insufficiency, but additional factors must be considered before deciding on an emergent C-section.", "role": "assistant"}, {"content": "They occurred a few times in the last 20 minutes mother is stable but I\u2019m not sure if we should rush to surgery", "role": "user"}, {"content": "Intermittent late decelerations can be mild or indicate the beginning of fetal compromise.", "role": "assistant"}, {"content": "I noticed some light vaginal bleeding not sure if it\u2019s connected", "role": "user"}, {"content": "Light vaginal bleeding can have multiple causes. If the fetal heart tracings remain consistently nonreassuring or the bleeding intensifies, evaluate for possible abruption.", "role": "assistant"}, {"content": "So do we move forward with a C-section or keep monitoring?", "role": "user"}]

note.text: light vaginal bleeding; etiology unclear, possible abruption
recurrent late decelerations on CTG over past 20 minutes — non-reassuring fetal status
